chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Whenever we want to render an array, for example if I have written my array like this,

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: then values are printed for us.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: But we want to display all our features in the form of elements or a list.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: In that case, we have to convert each individual array value into an item.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: We have two ways to do that.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: The first way is that when we are sending an array, we are sending values for it.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: That is, this is the individual value we sent, and this is the individual value we sent.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: So without sending individual values, we are sending an array of elements.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: For example, what does the word elements mean?

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: We remove it and pass our options to the array.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: For now, all the individual values are going, but what can we do?

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: We can send an array of elements, array of HTML elements.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: How? We can create an Ally element here, where we can write our text in the Ally element.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: And this Ally element will be a part of our array.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: It will be a value in our index.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: When we write a code in React, then it is possible to pass our values in the array in this way.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: We can create another Ally element here.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Let's remove this and write it here.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: And we will get another Ally element here, where we will get this thing.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Now, when we pass our options array directly, then it will not be a specific string.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: It will have a complete HTML element.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: And when we print our features in this paragraph in the product,

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: what will JSX do? JSX will remove one element of the array.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: What is in an array of an element? It is a complete list item.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: When the list is printed, the first list item will be removed, then it will be printed.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Then the second list item will be removed, then it will be printed.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Then the third list item will be removed, then it will be printed.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: So, in this way, when we want our values to be wrapped in an array element,

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: we can pre-wrap it in that element and send it.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: There is another way to do this, which we will use more frequently.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Which is, we have sent simple values from our product tab.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: The values that are going through the product tab are our simple strings.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: But when the values are going through the product, when we are printing the features,

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: then we will map the things for the entire array.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: The function of the map is to take an array element and change some things.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: So, we want to wrap the normal string element of the array in an Ally element.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: And this is what we want to repeat for each element of the array.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: So, we can use the map method for that.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: How will we use it? Here, we will make a new variable.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Let's call it list.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: And what will happen with list? We will map our features array.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: By mapping, we will take our individual feature.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: We can write this element as well. This is not features, this is feature.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: We will convert our individual feature to this Ally.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Which will have a different value for our feature.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: So, every feature value will be replaced to this element.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Which has an Ally element and a feature value for the Ally element.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: And this time, when we have to render, we will render our list directly.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Rather than the features array.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: We saved it, refreshed it. We will have this output.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: One thing you must have noticed, that the first thing we talked about about wheat is that wheat is faster and quicker.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Noticeably, you can see that change.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Because when we use nodemon or nodejs, we don't see the instant change.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: But here, all the changes in UI are much faster.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Now, we can copy this code directly here.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Generally, we write the logic here.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Rather than making a separate variable, we save it.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: And we will refresh it. That will also have the same output.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Now, there can be a map method. Some of the students missed out on revision time.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: So, revise the map method once.

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: Because in React, whenever we have to render the arrays, we will use the map method frequently.
